stories.title: EU countries eye setting up migrant ‘return hubs’ in Rwanda and Uzbekistan
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — A group of EU countries is considering sending rejected asylum seekers to Rwanda and Uzbekistan, three European diplomats confirmed, as the bloc moves to shift part of its migrant returns system beyond its borders for the first time. The plans follow the approval of a law  granting EU capitals the power to set up centers in non-EU countries for processing migrants who have been denied the right to stay in the bloc — so-called return hubs — provided those countries uphol

stories.title: Zelenskyy to skip Ukraine recovery conference amid spat with Poland
stories.summary: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko on Tuesday announced she will lead Kyiv’s delegation to this week’s Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdańsk, with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy set to skip the event amid a growing diplomatic dispute with his Polish counterpart, Karol Nawrocki. “I am leading Ukraine’s delegation and our overall work at the Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026 in Gdańsk,” wrote Svyrydenko on X . The Ukrainian team will include business leaders, officials, lawmakers and lo

stories.title: Assault on facts and truth led to Brexit | Letters
stories.summary: Readers reflect on the 2016 referendum and its lasting impact Winston Churchill once said : “No one pretends that democracy is perfect or all-wise. Indeed it has been said that democracy is the worst form of government except for all those other forms that have been tried from time to time.” The Brexit campaign was run by multimillionaires who loathed regulation, and they persuaded people who appeared to have not been educated about politics or economics at school, or received alternative views

stories.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy to skip postwar conference amid tensions with Poland
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president will not attend after sparking Polish ‘outrage’ over naming of military unit Volodymyr Zelenskyy will skip a high-level conference on the postwar reconstruction of Ukraine amid a deepening rift with Poland over his naming of a military unit after one that killed tens of thousands of Poles during the second world war. Ukraine’s president had been expected to co-host the Ukraine Recovery Conference, which begins in the Polish coastal city of Gdańsk on Thursday, but the Ukrainia

stories.title: Hungary hits pause on EU membership bids of Ukraine, Moldova
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Hungary has delayed a key procedural step needed to push forward the EU membership bids of Ukraine and Moldova, according to two EU diplomats. Kyiv and Chisinau reached a milestone on their path into the bloc when EU countries unanimously approved, on June 15, the opening of the first formal negotiating chapter for both countries. The move had been blocked for years by former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, who opposed Ukraine’s EU membership. (Ukraine and Moldova&#8

stories.title: Czech president sues prime minister over NATO attendance
stories.summary: Czech President Petr Pavel on Tuesday said that he had filed a lawsuit against the government, challenging Prime Minister Andrej Babiš’s decision to not include him in the government delegation attending the NATO summit in Ankara in July. The competency suit, which was lodged with Czechia’s Constitutional Court, asks for clarity on who has the authority to decide whether the head of state may attend the NATO summit. The court is due to take up the matter during its plenary session on

stories.title: Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal
stories.summary: Assailant behind shooting that left three people dead wrote ‘incel’-like manifesto that was posted by a far-right outlet Police in Canada are warning of possible copycat attacks after three people died in a shootout in Montreal and the assailant’s lengthy manifesto, which called for “a new bloodletting”, was posted online by a far-right outlet. The document contains many of the hallmark grievances of the “involuntary celibacy” – or “incel” – movement in addition racist and misogynistic conspirac

stories.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say
stories.summary: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicide Campaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her. Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. The single mother of daughters aged four and nine, was found on the fl

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens

stories.title: The Guardian view on the death of Carlo Ginzburg: a historian who taught us to think about outsiders | Editorial
stories.summary: The work of one of Italy’s greatest scholars focused on ordinary lives oppressed by power and prejudice. That approach resonates today Reflecting on the genesis of his most famous work, Carlo Ginzburg wrote that by immersing himself in the trial of a 16th-century miller burned by the Roman Inquisition, he turned a possible footnote into a book. stories.summary: A siklóernyős sértetlenül landolt egy fán, a segítségére siető honvédségi helikopter rotorszele miatt zuhant 20 métert. Egy hónappal később belehalt a sérüléseibe. A Magyar Honvédség szerint a férfi nagy mértékben hozzájárult ahhoz, hogy a baleset végül tragédiába fulladt, és vitatja, hogy kártérítést kellene fizetnie.

stories.summary: Almost every Hungarian female lawmaker has a story linked to sexism and has experienced online, sexual or psychological abuse, but none of them have reported it. A study covering five European countries found that Hungary’s poor results cannot be separated from how the Orbán regime operated.

stories.title: Fact-checking Brexit's key promises 10 years on – video

From immigration, a bus promising £350m a week for the NHS, to bendy bananas, the 'most consequential decision in modern history' had mixed results From bendy bananas to £350m for the NHS – how many Brexit promises actually came true? stories.title: Andy Burnham lines up ex-Blair minister James Purnell as chief of staff
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain’s likely next prime minister Andy Burnham is lining up James Purnell, who previously served as a minister under Tony Blair, as his chief of staff in 10 Downing Street. The proposed appointment is at an advanced stage as Burnham plans for his expected entry to No. 10 on July 17, two people with knowledge of the plan told POLITICO. One of the two people, granted anonymity to discuss internal planning, said they believed Purnell’s appointment was “pretty much locked on.” The Times

stories.title: Starmer government pauses new ‘major’ policy announcements

Starmer set out a timetable for his departure on Monday , announcing that Labour will begin a leadership contest in July to identify a successor. stories.title: Deaths linked to London air pollution have fallen 40%, study estimates
stories.summary: However, Imperial College team also find that pollution has worse health impact than previously understood Deaths linked to air pollution fell by an estimated 40% in London over the five years from 2019, according to new analysis. The city’s mayor, Sadiq Khan, welcomed what he called “overwhelming evidence” that his ultra-low emission zone was saving lives. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weakening UK net zero policy would damage economy, chief climate adviser says
stories.summary: Climate Change Committee chair Nigel Topping says U-turns damage investor confidence and disrupt businesses Weakening the UK’s net zero policy would disrupt business and damage the economy, the UK’s chief climate adviser has warned. Nigel Topping, chair of the Climate Change Committee (CCC), said: “The U-turns are really damaging to inward investor confidence. If we really want to grow the economy, then investing and getting good at building stuff is essential.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Man arrested near Trump’s reflecting pool plans to fight obscenity charge
stories.summary: Christian Miles berated Oklahoma state troopers near Washington DC landmark subject to botched renovation A Washington DC resident arrested this week near the National Mall’s reflecting pool told the Guardian he planned to fight the charges, as Donald Trump continues to blame vandals for the botched renovation of the pool. After the Trump administration spent $14.2m renovating thebody of water in front of the Lincoln Memorial to turn it “American flag” blue in time for the US’s 250th birthday ne

stories.title: House gives final approval to bipartisan housing bill aimed at lowering costs
stories.summary: Donald Trump has indicated he will sign rare bipartisan initiative to tackle affordability after 358-32 vote in House The House gave final approval on Tuesday to a broad bipartisan bill aimed at lowering the cost of housing, with lawmakers in both parties eager to show progress on affordability issues ahead of this year’s midterm elections. The 358-32 vote sends the bill to Donald Trump, who is expected to sign it into law on Wednesday at the Capitol. The Senate passed the legislation 85-5 on Mo

stories.title: Chinese supercomputer leapfrogs best US machines to be ranked world’s fastest
stories.summary: China’s LineShine debuts at number one in Top500 – a list sometimes viewed as a national measure of global tech prowess A supercomputer in China now outranks its US counterparts as the world’s most powerful. It is the first time since 2017 that a Chinese computer has topped a list sometimes viewed as a measure of a nation’s technological prowess. The LineShine computer in Shenzhen displaced top-ranked US computer El Capitan in the Top500 rankings released on Tuesday. It was LineShine’s debut on

stories.title: Three in five gen Z Britons would like new vote to rejoin EU, poll finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Data reveals 60% of 18 to 28-year-olds would vote to rejoin bloc if given the opportunity A generation of young Britons who were locked out of the 2016 EU referendum because of their age now believe that Brexit has failed, with a majority demanding a fresh vote to rejoin the EU, exclusive polling shows. Gen Z Britons show deep dissatisfaction with the UK’s departure from the EU, according to new polling of 18- to 28-year-olds conducted by the thinktank More in Common and shared with t

stories.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
stories.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

stories.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW
stories.summary: Before being imprisoned for 20 years, Jimmy Lai founded Hong Kong's largest pro-democracy newspaper. His son said the award shows that those who fight for the freedom of others "are never alone."

stories.title: Colombia: The 'Tiger's' victory signals a rightward shift
stories.summary: With the election of Abelardo de la Espriella, an ultra-right-wing outsider has won the presidential runoff in Colombia for the first time. The tight victory marks a political shift and could trigger new conflicts.

stories.title: US, Iran give conflicting claims over Hormuz fees
stories.summary: US top diplomat Rubio said Washington will not accept tolls on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. But Iran has said they want to charge "maritime service fees." And Israel and Lebanon are set for talks in the US.

stories.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
stories.summary: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

stories.title: Lebanon's lose-lose: Is there another way out of conflict?
stories.summary: The new US-Iran peace deal seems to be forcing Lebanon to choose between Israel's sphere of influence or Iran's. It wants neither. But could there be a better alternative, achieved with the help of regional diplomacy?

stories.title: Trains resume after radio issue resolved, Deutsche Bahn says
stories.summary: Train services across Germany have resumed following a technical meltdown on Tuesday night. But regional and suburban rail operators warned that continued delays were possible into Wednesday morning.

stories.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan agree landmark internet deal as peace process continues
stories.summary: Armenia and Azerbaijan’s leading telecom companies agreed to a joint internet transit project as both countries move forward together after decades of war, boosting regional connectivity.

stories.title: From Bolsonaro and Bukele to Fujimori and De la Espriella: Trump and Latin America's rightward turn
stories.summary: Soon after the US president first moved into the White House, his policies and outspoken backing for certain candidates inspired a new wave of Latin American leaders now shaping the continent’s future.

stories.title: US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks
stories.summary: The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair.

stories.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
stories.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

stories.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
stories.summary: Savannah Guthrie spoke on NBC's Today show about a note sent to the media in February regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, which claimed she had died.

stories.title: Have World Cup changes made final group stage games unfair?
stories.summary: With eight teams already having nothing to play for, has the jeopardy in the group stage at the World Cup been diminished? And are concerns over the integrity of competition justified?

stories.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
stories.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

stories.title: UN launches Hormuz evacuation for 11,000 stranded seafarers
stories.summary: The operation follows a peace agreement between the United States and Iran that ended months of conflict and allowed commercial shipping to resume through one of the world's most important maritime routes.

stories.title: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body
stories.summary: Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remained in dispute 12 months after he died.

stories.title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for state visit
stories.summary: The arrival of Japan's emperor and empress in Belgium marks a symbolic moment for two countries whose partnership stretches back more than a century and a half.

stories.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction
stories.summary: On Tuesday, Today show presenter Savannah Guthrie begged anyone with information to come forward, saying, "We are in agony."

stories.title: UNAIDS warns Trump's HIV funding cuts to South Africa could cost lives
stories.summary: The Trump administration says the phased withdrawal is linked to policy disputes with Pretoria, which rejects the allegations and says it is pursuing greater self-reliance.

stories.title: Belgian royals roll out red carpet for Japan's emperor
stories.summary: Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako received a ceremonial welcome at the Royal Palace of Brussels as they began a state visit to Belgium.

stories.title: Giant Messi statue honors Argentina's World Cup hero
stories.summary: A giant mural near Buenos Aires and a towering statue in Patagonia highlight Lionel Messi's enduring status as a symbol of unity and pride for Argentines during the World Cup.

stories.title: Airbus under fresh scrutiny as EASA orders A380 inspections over wing cracks
stories.summary: The aviation giant is already battling supply chain problems and growing costs across its operations.

stories.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

stories.title: Temperatures hit record levels in western Europe
stories.summary: France, Spain and Italy, have been hardest hit by the heatwave so far.

stories.title: Stanford was their golden ticket - could AI help or hinder that?
stories.summary: The BBC spoke with Stanford University graduates about what they really think about artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction

stories.title: Federal judge blocks Trump's push to use database to check citizenship status prior to voting
stories.summary: US federal court says the Trump-led initiative carries the risk of preventing valid voters from their constitutional right to democracy and tramples on Americans’ privacy.

stories.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children

stories.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.

stories.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
stories.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

stories.title: Who is Abelardo de la Espriella? From controversial lawyer to Colombia's president
stories.summary: The president-elect created his movement just 11 months ago, mirroring the model that worked in Argentina and El Salvador: patriotic talk, a firm hand against crime, and contempt for traditional politics.

stories.title: ‘I remember the shock’, ‘It can still be reversed’ – what do Europeans think of Brexit now?
stories.summary: After the 2016 referendum, panellists from other EU countries responded in the Guardian. Ten years on, we’ve gone back to them Read more from the Brexit Vote: 10 Years On series Continue reading...

stories.summary: The French artist is best known for his black-and-white images of high society, but a new exhibition shows us that he was also a bold and brilliant colour pioneer Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Geldof started flicking Vs at Farage’: the story of the Brexit campaign, told by those with a front-row seat
stories.summary: How five months in 2016 that encompassed Boris Johnson siding with Vote Leave, Jo Cox’s murder and David Cameron’s resignation shaped the UK’s future David Cameron, having promised in 2013 that a future Conservative government would offer a referendum on Britain’s membership of the EU , announces the date of the vote: 23 June 2016. The next day, Boris Johnson, then the mayor of London , says he will campaign for leave . Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe’s June heatwave – in pictures
stories.summary: The ‘heat dome’ settling over western Europe could bring temperatures of up to 40C to some parts of England and Wales in the middle part of this week Continue reading...

stories.title: EU faces fierce criticism over plans to host Taliban in Brussels
stories.summary: Rights campaigners and MEPs say meeting would normalise regime that erases women from public life Rights campaigners and MEPs have warned that a meeting between EU officials and a Taliban delegation in Brussels risks normalising a regime that has banned girls from school beyond the sixth grade and sought to erase women from public life, while its ranks include two leaders accused of crimes against humanity. A spokesperson for the Afghan foreign ministry confirmed that a delegation representing t

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

stories.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
stories.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
stories.summary: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful.

stories.title: EU hosts Taliban for migrant return talks in Brussels
stories.summary: The EU is hosting Taliban officials after Belgium granted temporary visas for talks on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers. The meeting is one of the bloc's highest-level contacts with the Taliban since 2021.

stories.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
stories.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

stories.title: Europe heat wave: France measures hottest-ever day
stories.summary: Monuments in Paris are working on reduced hours, and dozens of people in France have drowned during the heat wave. Britain, France, Italy and Spain have issued red alerts and health warnings. More on DW.

stories.title: Wanted: Jobs, critical minerals as Zambia's election nears
stories.summary: Zambians head to the polls in August with jobs and living costs at the top of their concerns. The outcome could also impact the ongoing global competition for critical minerals and redefine ties with China and the US.

stories.title: Afghan Taliban clamp down on women, smartphones, protests
stories.summary: The Taliban are tightening the screws on women's clothing, shooting at protesters and ordering even their own officials to stop using smartphones. Behind the regime's omnipresent control, there are signs of deep anxiety.

stories.title: Vance says direct talks with Iran set 'good foundation' for deal to end the war

stories.title: Kazakhstan brings New Silk Road revival to Brussels as Tokayev visits EU leaders

stories.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?

stories.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note said she died after abduction

stories.title: Brussels residents cool off in fountains as heatwave grips Belgium

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.title: Alpine glacier melts faster as second heatwave hits France
stories.summary: The Bossons Glacier in France’s Chamonix Valley is showing fresh signs of rapid retreat as a second heatwave of 2026 grips parts of Europe.

stories.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
stories.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

stories.title: Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia
stories.summary: Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented.

stories.title: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

stories.title: TV pundit apologises after racist comment during Belgium World Cup game
stories.summary: Bogdanović most notably played for La Liga side Atlético Madrid and Germany's Werder Bremen during his career.

stories.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
stories.summary: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

stories.title: 40 mayors worldwide unite to fight back against the AI data centre surge
stories.summary: From Phoenix to Melbourne, a global coalition of city leaders says the AI data centre boom is draining their power, water and land — and they are done waiting.

stories.title: Iran sharply contradicts the US, pushing its own view of framework deal
stories.summary: Shortly after the first high-level US-Iran negotiations ended, discrepancies emerged over what the two sides actually agreed, as Tehran moved to directly contradict US Vice President JD Vance’s statements, prompting US President Donald Trump to reject Iran's claims.

stories.title: Fire at animation studio in India kills at least 15 people
stories.summary: The fire happened in a building housing a student training centre in northern India, raising fresh concerns about fire safety in the country.

stories.title: From cool-down spots to chalk on windows - how Europe is coping with the heat

stories.summary: Fókuszban a könyvheti regények.

stories.summary: Hát, leginkább mert a saját válogatottjuk csapnivaló, de Maradona és Messi, meg a futball nemzetegyesítő ereje is szerepet játszanak benne.

stories.summary: A II. kerületi alpolgármester szerint főnöke továbbra is felelős az önkormányzat döntéseiért.

stories.summary: Ronaldo kétszer, Mendes és Leao egyszer-egyszer talált be, az üzbégek pedig csak öngólt rúgtak.

stories.summary: Jelenleg külön pénzfeldobás van az oldalválasztásra és az első rúgás jogára, a terv az, hogy egy legyen, ahol csak az egyiket lehet kiválasztani.

stories.summary: A Fidesz azzal vádolja a Tiszát, hogy pártirányítás alá vonták a rendőrséget és az ügyészséget.

stories.summary: Az ország délnyugati részén több mint 44 fokot is mértek, eddig már legalább 40 halálos áldozata van a hőhullámnak.

stories.summary: A portugál klasszis két gólt lőtt, nagyon hajtott a harmadikért is, de végül nem jött össze neki. Portugália viszont így is simán, 5-0-ra nyert, és a csoportelsőségért játszik majd Kolumbiával.

Trump láthatóan szabadulni akar a helyzetből, és annak örülhet, hogy a világgazdaság optimistán reagált a múlt heti megállapodásra. Ez azonban nem fedi el a fennmaradó problémákat.

stories.title: ‘Cruel hoax’: Canadian healthcare staff decry employer’s email promising fake day off
stories.summary: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and Labrador For years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point. So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

stories.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation
stories.summary: City’s police force faces investigation of 16 officers accused of disproportionately targeting Black and Arab residents Montreal’s mayor has called for a halt to random police checks as the city’s police force grapples with an internal investigation into racism and racial profiling by 16 officers. stories.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?

stories.title: 13 killed and 66 injured in explosion at Qatar’s largest energy site, authorities confirm
stories.summary: Qatar Energy announced on Monday afternoon that a massive accidental explosion and blaze at the country’s main energy site at Ras Laffan the night before killed 13 people and injured 66, as workers tried to resume operations. Qatar says its LNG exports are not affected.

stories.summary: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

stories.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
stories.summary: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira

stories.title: Legóból nem lehet 111-gyel száguldó, 1,7 tonnás szuperautót építeni? Lego: Fogd meg az építőkockámat!

stories.summary: A Trump-adminisztráció pár héttel ezelőtt nemzetbiztonsági aggályokra hivatkozva tett be a cégnek, most kiderült, hogy egy belső teszt eredményei miatt ijedhettek meg.

stories.title: Egy jordán futballszurkoló meghalt a meccsnézéskor összepréselődő tömegben
stories.summary: Ammánban az Algéria elleni meccsre gyűltek össze a szurkolók, de lökdösődés alakult ki, végül kilencen kerültek kórházba.

stories.title: Brutális kánikula jön: meghosszabbították és péntek éjféltől harmadfokúra emelték a hőségriasztást
stories.summary: Pénteken és szombaton 38, vasárnap 39, hétfőn pedig akár már 40 fok is lehet a legfrissebb előrejelzések szerint.

stories.title: Elfogták a rendőrök a férfit, aki az Erzsébet hídról a Dunába dobálta a Pride-zászlókat
stories.summary: Rongálás gyanúja miatt hallgatták ki.

stories.title: Valóban pattognak a petefészkek és erősödnek az erekciók a sertéshústól?
stories.summary: Az agrártárca több sertéshús fogyasztására szólította fel a lakosságot, mondván, ettől nemcsak általában leszünk egészségesebbek, de a termékenységnek is jót tesz. Igaz ez? Kell a tányérunkra több sertéshús?

stories.title: Varga Mihály: Az árrésstopok azonnali kivezetése sem veszélyeztetné az árstabilitást
stories.summary: A jegybankelnök szerint mini kamatcsökkentési ciklusba kezdtek, ami a nyár folyamán még több kamatvágással jár majd, aztán ősszel meglátják, hogyan tovább.

stories.title: A Lime nem közlekedési cégként próbál minél több pénzt bevonni
stories.summary: A társaság a Nasdaq-ra vezeti be részvényeit, értéke 1,66 milliárd dollár is lehet, holott még egyetlen cent adózott nyereséget sem termelt. Kérdés, hogy eléggé technológiai cég-e a befektetők szemében.

stories.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
stories.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

stories.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
stories.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

stories.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
stories.summary: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
stories.summary: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

stories.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
stories.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

stories.title: Lower-level technical talks to continue as first round of direct US-Iran negotiations wrap up
stories.summary: Iran’s top diplomat hailed mediators Pakistan and Qatar’s efforts over what he called “major progress” to end the continued Israeli offensive on Lebanon, despite a fragile truce. The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

stories.title: Spain pound Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Egypt tops Group G in matchday two fixtures of the 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Spain returns to its winning ways as Egypt break their group’s draw deadlock in the second matchday fixtures for groups G and H at the 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Australian police seize nearly 3 tonnes of cocaine in what has become the largest bust on record
stories.summary: The bust of nearly 3 tonnes is the country’s largest ever and is believed to hold a street value of just shy of €500 million.

stories.title: Norway fans take over Times Square with Viking row
stories.summary: Norwegian supporters in Viking helmets and national colours packed Times Square, chanting and singing before Norway’s Group I match against Senegal.

stories.title: China announces sanctions on 10 US companies as trade tensions flare
stories.summary: The sanctions come a month after US President Donald Trump visited Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping aimed at stabilising relations.

stories.title: Canal Saint-Martin becomes cooling spot as heatwave grips Paris
stories.summary: Paris faces extreme heat as crowds use fountains and the Canal Saint-Martin to cool off, while authorities impose alerts and safety measures.

stories.title: World's largest kite festival fills Danish skies with 25,000 kites
stories.summary: Thousands gather at Denmark's Fano International Kite Festival as giant dragon, whale and octopus kites fill the skies above the coast.

stories.title: Iran and US make progress on Hormuz despite Trump's explosive threats
stories.summary: JD Vance said 18 hours of talks in Switzerland had "laid a very good foundation" after both sides agreed a mechanism to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and maintain Lebanon's ceasefire, even as Trump threatened to hit Iran "only harder".

stories.title: 'Another day, another leader': Londoners react to Starmer's resignation
stories.summary: People in central London expressed frustration, uncertainty and cautious hope after Keir Starmer announced his resignation as UK prime minister.

stories.title: Red heat alerts issued in France, Italy and Spain as 40C temperatures forecast
stories.summary: The heatwave conditions are forecast to intensify in the coming days across central and western Europe.

stories.title: Japan to increase visa fees by more than five times starting on 1 July
stories.summary: Japan wants to help manage the rising costs of its immigration system by making tourist visas significantly more expensive in the first fee increase in nearly half a century.

stories.title: Messi smashes all-time World Cup goalscoring record with 18 goals for Argentina
stories.summary: Messi has become the highest-scoring player in World Cup history with 18 goals. He now has two more than the previous record holder, former Germany striker Miroslav Klose.

stories.title: Belgium issues visas to Taliban delegation for EU migration talks
stories.summary: The EU is preparing to host Taliban representatives for the first time since the group's return to power in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Honduras to buy Ukrainian drones to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: The discussions come as the Central American country continues to grapple with drug trafficking and some of the highest levels of violence in the region.
